questions.q: What is the primary function of a router in a network?
opts: (A) To connect multiple networks
(B) To provide internet access
(C) To manage network devices
(D) To store data
questions.explanation: A router connects multiple networks by forwarding data packets between them. It does not provide internet access, manage network devices, or store data.

questions.q: What is the difference between a hub and a switch?
opts: (A) A hub is faster than a switch
(B) A hub is more expensive than a switch
(C) A hub broadcasts data to all devices, while a switch forwards data to specific devices
(D) A hub is used for wired connections, while a switch is used for wireless connections
questions.explanation: A hub broadcasts data to all connected devices, while a switch forwards data to the specific device it's intended for. This makes a switch more efficient and secure.

questions.q: What is the purpose of ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) in a network?
opts: (A) To convert IP addresses to MAC addresses
(B) To convert MAC addresses to IP addresses
(C) To resolve domain names to IP addresses
(D) To resolve IP addresses to domain names
questions.explanation: ARP is used to convert IP addresses to MAC addresses, which is necessary for data transmission between devices on a network.

questions.q: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
opts: (A) TCP is connection-oriented, while UDP is connectionless
(B) TCP is connectionless, while UDP is connection-oriented
(C) TCP is faster than UDP
(D) TCP is more reliable than UDP
questions.explanation: TCP is a connection-oriented protocol, meaning it establishes a connection before data transmission, while UDP is a connectionless protocol, meaning it does not establish a connection before data transmission.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the SYN flag in TCP?
opts: (A) To request a connection
(B) To acknowledge a connection
(C) To terminate a connection
(D) To retransmit data
questions.explanation: The SYN flag is used to request a connection in the three-way handshake process of TCP.

questions.q: What is the difference between a LAN and a WAN?
opts: (A) A LAN is a network that spans a single building, while a WAN is a network that spans multiple buildings
(B) A LAN is a network that spans multiple buildings, while a WAN is a network that spans a single building
(C) A LAN is a network that connects a small number of devices, while a WAN is a network that connects a large number of devices
(D) A LAN is a network that connects a large number of devices, while a WAN is a network that connects a small number of devices
questions.explanation: A LAN is a network that spans a single building or a small geographic area, while a WAN is a network that spans multiple buildings or a large geographic area.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a subnet mask in IP addressing?
opts: (A) To identify the network ID
(B) To identify the host ID
(C) To determine the subnet size
(D) To determine the broadcast address
questions.explanation: A subnet mask is used to identify the network ID in IP addressing.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Class A, Class B, and Class C IP address?
opts: (A) Class A has a maximum of 256 hosts, Class B has a maximum of 65,536 hosts, and Class C has a maximum of 16,777,216 hosts
(B) Class A has a maximum of 65,536 hosts, Class B has a maximum of 256 hosts, and Class C has a maximum of 16,777,216 hosts
(C) Class A has a maximum of 16,777,216 hosts, Class B has a maximum of 256 hosts, and Class C has a maximum of 65,536 hosts
(D) Class A has a maximum of 256 hosts, Class B has a maximum of 65,536 hosts, and Class C has a maximum of 16,777,216 hosts
questions.explanation: Class A has a maximum of 256 hosts, Class B has a maximum of 65,536 hosts, and Class C has a maximum of 16,777,216 hosts.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a DNS (Domain Name System) server?
opts: (A) To resolve IP addresses to domain names
(B) To resolve domain names to IP addresses
(C) To manage network devices
(D) To store data
questions.explanation: A DNS server is used to resolve domain names to IP addresses, making it easier to access websites and other online resources.

questions.q: What is the difference between a hub and a repeater?
opts: (A) A hub is used for wireless connections, while a repeater is used for wired connections
(B) A hub is used for wired connections, while a repeater is used for wireless connections
(C) A hub broadcasts data to all connected devices, while a repeater forwards data to specific devices
(D) A hub is used to amplify signal strength, while a repeater is used to extend network range
questions.explanation: A hub is used to broadcast data to all connected devices, while a repeater is used to amplify signal strength and extend network range.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a proxy server?
opts: (A) To cache frequently accessed web pages
(B) To filter incoming and outgoing network traffic
(C) To provide internet access to multiple devices
(D) To manage network devices
questions.explanation: A proxy server is used to filter incoming and outgoing network traffic, helping to protect the network from malware and unauthorized access.

questions.q: What is the difference between a firewall and an access control list (ACL)?
opts: (A) A firewall is used to filter incoming traffic, while an ACL is used to filter outgoing traffic
(B) A firewall is used to filter outgoing traffic, while an ACL is used to filter incoming traffic
(C) A firewall is used to block malicious traffic, while an ACL is used to allow authorized traffic
(D) A firewall is used to allow authorized traffic, while an ACL is used to block malicious traffic
questions.explanation: A firewall is used to filter outgoing traffic, while an ACL is used to filter incoming traffic.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a DMZ (Demilitarized Zone)?
opts: (A) To provide external access to internal network resources
(B) To block external access to internal network resources
(C) To cache frequently accessed web pages
(D) To manage network devices
questions.explanation: A DMZ is used to provide external access to internal network resources, while still maintaining security and segregation.

questions.q: What is the difference between a VPN (Virtual Private Network) and a VAN (Value-Added Network)?
opts: (A) A VPN provides secure remote access to a network, while a VAN provides secure remote access to a cloud service
(B) A VPN provides secure remote access to a cloud service, while a VAN provides secure remote access to a network
(C) A VPN is used to connect multiple sites, while a VAN is used to connect multiple devices
(D) A VPN is used to connect multiple devices, while a VAN is used to connect multiple sites
questions.explanation: A VPN provides secure remote access to a network, while a VAN is not a commonly used term and seems unrelated to the question. VPN is used for secure remote access, and VAN seems a distractor.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a network switch in a network?
opts: (A) To provide internet access
(B) To connect multiple networks
(C) To manage network devices
(D) To forward data packets between networks
questions.explanation: A network switch is used to forward data packets between networks.

questions.q: What is the difference between a network interface card (NIC) and a network interface controller (NICX)?
opts: (A) A NIC is used for wired connections, while a NICX is used for wireless connections
(B) A NIC is used for wireless connections, while a NICX is used for wired connections
(C) A NIC is a hardware component, while a NICX is a software component
(D) A NIC is a software component, while a NICX is a hardware component
questions.explanation: A NIC is used for wired connections, while a NICX is not a commonly used term and seems unrelated to the question. NIC is a hardware component and used for wired connections, and NICX seems a distractor.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a network bridge in a network?
opts: (A) To connect multiple networks
(B) To provide internet access
(C) To manage network devices
(D) To forward data packets between networks
questions.explanation: A network bridge is used to connect multiple networks.

questions.q: What is the difference between a network hub and a network concentrator?
opts: (A) A hub is used to connect multiple devices, while a concentrator is used to connect multiple networks
(B) A hub is used to connect multiple networks, while a concentrator is used to connect multiple devices
(C) A hub is used to amplify signal strength, while a concentrator is used to extend network range
(D) A hub is used to extend network range, while a concentrator is used to amplify signal strength
questions.explanation: A hub is used to connect multiple devices, while a concentrator is used to connect multiple networks.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a network repeater in a network?
opts: (A) To connect multiple networks
(B) To provide internet access
(C) To manage network devices
(D) To amplify signal strength and extend network range
questions.explanation: A network repeater is used to amplify signal strength and extend network range.

questions.q: What is the difference between a network cable and a network adapter?
opts: (A) A cable is used to connect devices, while an adapter is used to connect networks
(B) A cable is used to connect networks, while an adapter is used to connect devices
(C) A cable is a hardware component, while an adapter is a software component
(D) A cable is a software component, while an adapter is a hardware component
questions.explanation: A network cable is used to connect devices, while a network adapter is not a commonly used term and seems unrelated to the question. Network cable is a hardware component and used for connecting devices, and network adapter seems a distractor.
